title: World-Arc Quest
description: SNG-203 tier 1. A quest that is a player's HANDLE on a greater arc (greater_arcs.json). Its resolution advances the named arc's stage on the SHARED clock — every other player's next load sees the world moved. Extends quest_structure.json (all its required fields still apply: premise, stakes, axis, traditions, stages, routes, outcomes, consequences) and ADDS the arc-binding + shared-broadcast fields. The distinguishing effect type is `arc_stage`: a world_event with teeth that moves greater_arcs.json[arcId].currentStage. GM can generate new world-arc quests via generate('world_arc_quest', ctx) against this schema, binding to any active arc that has a stage ladder.

id.description: stable slug; generated arcs mint via the SNG-019 resolver

arcId.description: the greater_arcs.json arc this quest is a handle on. MUST reference an existing arc id.

arcStageFrom.description: the arc stage this quest departs FROM. Requires the arc to carry a numbered stage ladder (SNG-203 §7 authors these onto the 5 greater arcs).

arcStageTo.description: the arc stage a completing outcome moves the arc TO. May be lower than From — a world arc CAN move backward (contested advancement is a feature, §3).

stakes.description: what is at risk and WHO PAYS if the player walks away. Mandatory. At world scale, the 'who pays' is often everyone. (quest_structure.json)

axis.description: the tension-axis this quest lives on. (quest_structure.json)

condition.description: ENGINE-TESTABLE: place reached / person spoken to / thing obtained / truth learned / roll passed. Never vague 'investigate further'.

change.description: what shifts in the world when this stage lands

routes.description: tradition-keyed approaches; a quest with one solution is a lock, not a story. (quest_structure.json)

outcomes.description: 2-4 BRANCHED endings — which success, not success/fail. At least one outcome MUST carry an `arc_stage` effect (that is what makes this a world-arc quest and not a regional one).

effects.description: machine-readable deltas. Same vocabulary as quest_structure.json PLUS `arc_stage`. At least one durable+findable effect required; at least one outcome must include an `arc_stage`.

sharedBroadcast.description: world-arc outcomes are broadcast to the shared clock — every player's next load sees the change. Distinguishes an arc move (public) from a personal codex fact (private). Default true; an `arc_stage` effect implies it.

publicFace.description: the ONE-LINE public statement of what visibly changed, for the shared 'state of the world' surface (§3). NEVER contains the arc's GM-EYES-ONLY truth — only what any observer would see. Rating-lens applies.

designLaws: A world-arc quest MUST move a real arc. If arcStageFrom == arcStageTo across all outcomes, it is a regional quest wearing a world costume — reject.
The public face is mandatory and spoiler-free: the shared surface shows THAT the world moved and what an observer sees, never WHY at the truth layer.
Contested advancement is legitimate: two players can move one arc opposite ways. Backward motion is a feature and must read as one.
Everything quest_structure.json's designLaws require (no clean exit, never punish the route, findable later, someone permanently changed) still holds — a world quest is a quest first.
